text: Can you.. concisely summarize what Noosphere is? Skimming their public-facing material and it's all a bit.. inscrutable.

elements.text: Can you.. concisely summarize what Noosphere is? Skimming their public-facing material and it's all a bit.. inscrutable.

text: I have noose fear, sure, but who doesn't?

elements.text: I have noose fear, sure, but who doesn't?

text:
I can't quite tell either. My best guess: it's the idea of sister wikis (<https://wiki.c2.com/?AboutSisterSites|https://wiki.c2.com/?AboutSisterSites>) rethought for a world 20 years later, with IPFS and a few other things.

Mostly I'm there because Gordon Brander posts fun links.

text:
I saw Jerry facilitate conversations on stage at a TfT one-day conf at Betaworks NYC, Gordan Brander had presented Noosphere too.

Jerry and his 2nd brain are seamless. It's kind of amazing to watch live. He's been at it for so long that there really is decades of connections at his fingertips and he uses the brain to deepen the conversation and the conversation to add to the brain, and he's able to do it without breaking presence/flow somehow.

elements.text:
I saw Jerry facilitate conversations on stage at a TfT one-day conf at Betaworks NYC, Gordan Brander had presented Noosphere too.

Jerry and his 2nd brain are seamless. It's kind of amazing to watch live. He's been at it for so long that there really is decades of connections at his fingertips and he uses the brain to deepen the conversation and the conversation to add to the brain, and he's able to do it without breaking presence/flow somehow.

text:
Highly recommend!

The problem is an old and wonderful one -- how do you make it easier to get started with programming languages? (e.g. COBOL from the 60's where you describe arithmetic in words?) The desire to be “forgiving” of minor errors comes at the expense of precision of expression, and Hedy meets this head on!

They are deliberately matching this expression-precision trade off with well defined "levels" for learners to gain confidence without big jumps, scary failures, or large cognitive loads. Every time you discover a lack-of-precision need you discover a syntactic solution, and level up.

In essence, "how do you lower the floor while keeping the ceiling high?"

elements.text:
Highly recommend!

The problem is an old and wonderful one -- how do you make it easier to get started with programming languages? (e.g. COBOL from the 60's where you describe arithmetic in words?) The desire to be “forgiving” of minor errors comes at the expense of precision of expression, and Hedy meets this head on!

They are deliberately matching this expression-precision trade off with well defined "levels" for learners to gain confidence without big jumps, scary failures, or large cognitive loads. Every time you discover a lack-of-precision need you discover a syntactic solution, and level up.

In essence, "how do you lower the floor while keeping the ceiling high?"

text:
They’re also tackling A LOT of natural languages (the ability to do so is enabled by the levels), and there’s some fun parsing challenges with that.

Recording of Felienne’s talk is here! <https://www.twitch.tv/videos/1719122823|https://www.twitch.tv/videos/1719122823>
